verdict_note: Strict numerical: NO-GO (Tc < 106 K). Nuanced: CONDITIONAL because the shortfall is due to KNOWN phonon-only limitation in cuprates, not a pipeline bug. Pipeline mechanics are validated.
verdict_reasoning: Phonon-only Eliashberg gives Tc = 31.4 K (mu*=0.10) to 27.4 K (mu*=0.13), far below the 106 K CONDITIONAL threshold. However, this is the EXPECTED result for phonon-only Eliashberg in cuprates: published calculations for YBCO, La214, and Hg1201 give Tc ~ 30-55 K from phonon coupling alone. The ~80% discrepancy is not a pipeline failure but a known limitation of the phonon-only approach for cuprate superconductors, where spin fluctuations contribute significantly to pairing. The pipeline mechanics (structure -> phonons -> alpha2F -> lambda -> Tc) are validated: lambda = 1.19 is physically reasonable, alpha2F has correct spectral features, and all internal consistency checks pass. Verdict: NO-GO for phonon-only pipeline matching 151 K. CONDITIONAL for pipeline mechanics validation with documented offset.
recommendation: The phonon-mediated Eliashberg pipeline is mechanically sound but physically incomplete for cuprates. For Phases 28-30 (new structure predictions): (1) For hydride and conventional SC candidates, the pipeline should work with ~10% accuracy, as demonstrated by H3S and LaH10 benchmarks. (2) For cuprate candidates, add a systematic phonon-channel offset: the pipeline captures ~20% of the total Tc. Use this as a LOWER BOUND on Tc. (3) For more accurate cuprate Tc, implement spin-fluctuation Eliashberg (e.g., FLEX + Eliashberg or DFT+DMFT + Eliashberg). (4) Do NOT tune mu* to match experiment -- this is explicitly forbidden.
